mnemonics: PNE (Pure Nash Equilibrium) - Players Never Experiment (they stick to one strategy)
keyPoints: Understand the concept of Nash Equilibrium in game theory
Learn how algorithms can compute equilibria in games
Study the applications of algorithmic game theory in mechanism design and auctions
commonMistakes: Confusing Nash Equilibrium with other solution concepts like Pareto optimality
Assuming all games have a pure strategy Nash equilibrium
Overlooking computational complexity when designing game-theoretic algorithms
